title: Who is the most well written character Asuka or Shinji
display_title: Who is the most well written character Asuka or Shinji
wikitext:
[]: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by IpPo2911 » Sat Jan 25, 2025 6:16 pm I really get mad when people confuse preference with quality LOL. Shinji is unbearable and always will be, but there’s NO WAY anyone could think he’s a bad character (he’s literally one of the best anime protagonists ever in terms of quality). Anyway, Shinji or Asuka—who’s better written? I can’t decide, so I’m leaving it to you guys.

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sun Jan 26, 2025 2:37 pm Shinji definitely appeals more to the ideology and concepts behind Evangelion, making him the embodiment of self-doubt and existentialism. Asuka is also an extremely well-written character who changed the pacing and mood of the series as a whole, making a huge impression due to her antagonism and rivalry against the other pilots, something that you don't come across for supporting characters, or not in a way that is so humorous and exaggerated way. However, I feel the direction of the series did change around episode 16 to make it more of a "Shinji is a loner" rather than "Shinji is a lover" type anime, since that's just the nature of the story that was planned, so characters like Asuka had to be sidelined for Shinji's development and character development. The situation reminds me of Lucy from Cyberpunk edgerunners, who had a great role at the beginning but just becomes rather absent and depressed in later episodes. Overall, I believe Asuka had the potential to have a bigger role in the series as she was written in a way that made it more entertaining. When considering the core aspects of battling angels for the sake of humanity or love triangles, she perhaps has a more superior construct to Shinji. Regarding philosophy of depression, Shinji is always the archetypal construct.

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Sun Jan 26, 2025 4:46 pm dzzthink wrote: Overall, I believe Asuka had the potential to have a bigger role in the series as she was written in a way that made it more entertaining. When considering the core aspects of battling angels for the sake of humanity or love triangles, she perhaps has a more superior construct to Shinji. Regarding philosophy of depression, Shinji is always the archetypal construct.

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Tue Jan 28, 2025 5:29 am C.T.1290 wrote: What would Asuka be like if she was the main protagonist in the place of Shinji, like what Anno had originally planned? Would things be more different?

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Tue Jan 28, 2025 5:52 pm dzzthink wrote: The character of a shy introvert seems more suited for a male character though, especially since Anno was speaking from his own personal history, so for realism it makes more sense that Shinji was a boy.

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Feb 05, 2025 9:16 am Shinji is more explored, indeed. But to be honest with you, I think Asuka is just as well written as he is. For a character to appear mid-story and still have such an impact is no small feat. While yes, she ended up complementing Shinji's story, she still has a side story of her own. Other characters like Rei and Gendo have stories connected to Shinji's, but hers is different. Asuka's story has a beginning, middle, and end that all work to her character's advantage. It is a self-contained journey that leaves a unique impact on the viewer while also advancing the overall themes of Evangelion. Asuka, alongside Shinji, has become a symbol of Evangelion. That's why you always hear people talking about Shinji and Asuka. They're both great characters.

[]: Re: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Wed Feb 05, 2025 4:22 pm ^So I take that these two complement each other, with both of them being similar in almost every way. Although I tend to sympathize with Shinji more than I do with Asuka, which might have been the show’s intention. I wonder how different things would be for me if it was the other way around.
summary: []: Who is the most well written character: Asuka or Shinji?             Quote Post by IpPo2911 » Sat Jan 25, 2025 6:16 pm I really get mad when people confuse preference with quality LOL. Shinji is unbearable and always will be, but there’s NO WAY anyone could think he’s a bad character (he’s literally one of the best anime protagonists ever in terms of quality). Anyway, Shinji or Asuka—who’s better written? I can’t decide, so I’m leaving
